definition: Gopinath's dominant currency paradigm establishes that trade invoicing predominantly in USD creates a co-movement channel across currencies during stress episodes. Dollar invoicing share has remained stable even as reserve allocation has shifted, making it the most persistent dimension of dollar dominance.
evidence: USD involved in 88% of FX turnover, unchanged over 20 years (BIS Triennial)
Invoicing share stable vs declining reserve allocation share
Provides the mechanism for carry-momentum correlation regime switch
open_questions: Whether CIPS growth could eventually erode dollar invoicing dominance
Speed at which invoicing patterns can shift vs reserve allocation patterns
